dependencies.relationship: Modifies the provider registration switch in main.go startup sequence (spec 01_server_foundation group 3)
Implements the Provider interface and uses the Registry defined in spec 02_oauth_and_users

task_groups.title: Write GoogleProvider unit tests — constructor, AuthorizeURL, and URL overrides

subtasks.title: Scaffold google_provider_test.go with package declaration and imports
details: Create `internal/auth/google_provider_test.go` with package `auth` (or `auth_test`).
Import `net/http`, `net/http/httptest`, `testing`, and any internal packages needed.
Add a compile-time interface assertion: `var _ Provider = (*GoogleProvider)(nil)`.
Leave test function stubs (returning t.Skip or failing) to be filled in subsequent subtasks.

subtasks.title: Write tests for NewGoogleProvider defaults (TS-07-2) and URL overrides (TS-07-3)
details: TestNewGoogleProvider_Defaults: call NewGoogleProvider with empty URL fields; assert AuthorizeURL, TokenURL, UserInfoURL, and Scopes match documented defaults.
TestNewGoogleProvider_URLOverrides: call NewGoogleProvider with all three URL overrides set; assert each overridden URL is preserved.
Use table-driven sub-tests where appropriate.

subtasks.title: Write tests for SetHTTPClient injection (TS-07-4) and empty-credentials edge case (TS-07-E1)
details: TestSetHTTPClient: create a mock httptest.Server, call SetHTTPClient with its client, trigger an HTTP method, and assert the mock server received the request.
TestNewGoogleProvider_EmptyCredentials: call NewGoogleProvider with empty ClientID and ClientSecret; assert non-nil provider is returned with defaults applied (no panic).
Confirm the provider compiles and is non-nil.

subtasks.title: Write test for AuthorizeURL query parameters (TS-07-5)
details: TestAuthorizeURL: call p.AuthorizeURL("random-state-string", "http://localhost:8080/callback").
Parse the returned URL string with `url.Parse`.
Assert scheme+host+path equals the default authorize URL.
Assert query params: response_type=code, client_id, redirect_uri, state, scope (contains openid, email, profile).

checks: All test stubs in `internal/auth/google_provider_test.go` compile without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
Compile-time interface check `var _ Provider = (*GoogleProvider)(nil)` compiles (will fail until group 3 implementation, but file itself is syntactically valid)
Test file is properly formatted: `gofmt -l internal/auth/google_provider_test.go` produces no output
No linter issues in test file: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`

task_groups.title: Write GoogleProvider unit tests — ExchangeCode happy path and error paths

subtasks.title: Write ExchangeCode success test with grant_type assertion (TS-07-6, TS-07-20)
details: TestExchangeCode_Success: create httptest.Server returning HTTP 200 with `{"access_token": "tok123"}`.
Capture incoming request inside the handler.
Assert: returned token == "tok123", err == nil.
Assert request: method == POST, Content-Type == application/x-www-form-urlencoded.
Assert body fields: grant_type=authorization_code, client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri.

subtasks.title: Write ExchangeCode error tests — non-2xx (TS-07-7) and invalid JSON (TS-07-8)
details: TestExchangeCode_Non2xx: mock server returns HTTP 401; assert token=="" and err.Error() contains "401".
TestExchangeCode_InvalidJSON: mock server returns HTTP 200 with body "not-json"; assert token=="" and err!=nil.
Use table-driven tests if feasible.

subtasks.title: Write ExchangeCode network error edge case tests (TS-07-E2, TS-07-E3)
details: TestExchangeCode_Timeout: configure httptest.Server that never responds; inject http.Client with 10ms timeout; assert ExchangeCode returns error containing "timeout" or "deadline" within bounded time.
TestExchangeCode_ConnectionRefused: configure TokenURL to an unused port (e.g. 127.0.0.1:1); assert error contains "connection refused" or "dial".

checks: Test functions compile without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
No linter issues: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`
Test file is properly formatted: `gofmt -l internal/auth/google_provider_test.go` produces no output
Requirements 07-REQ-3.1, 07-REQ-3.2, 07-REQ-3.3, 07-REQ-8.2 acceptance criteria are covered by assertions in test stubs

task_groups.title: Write GoogleProvider unit tests — GetUserInfo, username derivation, and property/edge-case tests

subtasks.title: Write GetUserInfo success and field-mapping tests (TS-07-9, TS-07-10)
details: TestGetUserInfo_Success: httptest.Server returns HTTP 200 with valid userinfo JSON; assert err==nil, info!=nil, all fields non-empty, Authorization header == "Bearer valid-token".

TestGetUserInfo_FieldMapping: server returns `{"id":"117730543842840592312","email":"jane.doe+work@gmail.com","verified_email":true,"name":"Jane Doe"}`; assert info.ID, info.Login, info.Email, info.Name exactly.

subtasks.title: Write GetUserInfo error path tests (TS-07-11, TS-07-12) and network edge cases (TS-07-E4, TS-07-E5)
details: TestGetUserInfo_Non2xx: server returns HTTP 403; assert (nil, error) and error contains "403".
TestGetUserInfo_InvalidJSON: server returns HTTP 200 with "<html>not json</html>"; assert (nil, error).
TestGetUserInfo_Timeout: hanging server + 10ms client timeout; assert timeout/deadline error within bounded time.
TestGetUserInfo_ConnectionRefused: UserInfoURL pointed at unused port; assert network error.

subtasks.title: Write username derivation tests (TS-07-13, TS-07-14, TS-07-15, TS-07-E6, TS-07-E7)
details: TestUsernameDerivation_CanonicalExample: email "jane.doe+work@gmail.com" → Login=="janedoework".
TestUsernameDerivation_Truncation: email with 41+ alphanumeric local part chars → Login length == 39.
TestUsernameDerivation_Empty: email "...@gmail.com" → GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with descriptive message.
TestUsernameDerivation_NoAtSign: email "invalidemail" → (nil, error).
TestUsernameDerivation_EmptyLocalPart: email "@gmail.com" → (nil, error).
Assert Login matches regexp `^[0-9A-Za-z-]+$` for all success cases.

subtasks.title: Write property-based tests for username derivation and URL defaults (TS-07-P1 through TS-07-P4)
details: Use Go's `testing/quick` or a table-driven approach with representative email samples to cover TS-07-P1 (Login always valid when GetUserInfo succeeds).
TS-07-P2: generate OAuthProvider structs with empty URLs, assert defaults always applied.
TS-07-P3: generate arbitrary (code, redirectURI) pairs, assert grant_type always present in captured request.
TS-07-P4: apply sanitization helper to arbitrary emails with ≥1 alphanumeric/hyphen in local part; assert length 1–39 and charset.

checks: All test functions in group 3 compile without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
No linter issues: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`
Test file is properly formatted: `gofmt -l internal/auth/google_provider_test.go` produces no output
Requirements 07-REQ-4.1 through 07-REQ-5.E2 acceptance criteria are each covered by at least one test assertion

task_groups.title: Implement GoogleProvider struct, constructor, and SetHTTPClient

subtasks.title: Create internal/auth/google_provider.go with GoogleProvider struct and default constants
details: Create `internal/auth/google_provider.go` in package `auth`.
Define constants: `googleDefaultAuthorizeURL`, `googleDefaultTokenURL`, `googleDefaultUserInfoURL`, `googleDefaultScopes`.
Define `GoogleProvider` struct with fields: `clientID`, `clientSecret`, `authorizeURL`, `tokenURL`, `userInfoURL`, `scopes string`, `httpClient *http.Client`.
Add compile-time interface assertion: `var _ Provider = (*GoogleProvider)(nil)`.

subtasks.title: Implement NewGoogleProvider constructor with URL defaulting logic
details: Implement `func NewGoogleProvider(cfg OAuthProvider) *GoogleProvider`.
Apply defaults for AuthorizeURL, TokenURL, UserInfoURL when the config fields are empty strings.
Always set Scopes to `openid email profile`.
Set httpClient to `http.DefaultClient` initially.
Return the constructed `*GoogleProvider` without error regardless of empty credentials.

subtasks.title: Implement SetHTTPClient and AuthorizeURL methods
details: Implement `func (p *GoogleProvider) SetHTTPClient(client *http.Client)` that replaces `p.httpClient`.
Implement `func (p *GoogleProvider) AuthorizeURL(state, redirectURI string) string`.
Use `url.Values` to build query params: client_id, redirect_uri, response_type=code, scope, state.
Parse the base authorizeURL with `url.Parse`, attach query params, and return the full URL string.

checks: Package compiles without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
Compile-time interface assertion `var _ Provider = (*GoogleProvider)(nil)` does not trigger a build error (will verify once all methods are stubbed/implemented)
No linter issues: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`
Tests TS-07-2, TS-07-3, TS-07-4, TS-07-5 pass: `go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -run 'TestNewGoogleProvider|TestSetHTTPClient|TestAuthorizeURL'`
Requirements 07-REQ-1.1 through 07-REQ-2.1 acceptance criteria met

subtasks.title: Implement ExchangeCode — build and POST form-encoded request body
details: Implement `func (p *GoogleProvider) ExchangeCode(code, redirectURI string) (string, error)`.
Build `url.Values` with: grant_type=authorization_code, client_id, client_secret, code, redirect_uri.
POST to `p.tokenURL` using `p.httpClient` with `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.
Ensure response body is closed with `defer resp.Body.Close()` in all paths.

subtasks.title: Implement ExchangeCode — response parsing and error handling
details: On non-2xx status, return `("", fmt.Errorf("google token endpoint returned status %d", resp.StatusCode))`.
Decode JSON response into a struct with `AccessToken string` field.
If JSON decode fails or access_token is empty, return `("", error)` with descriptive message.
On HTTP client errors (network failure, timeout), propagate the error directly from `p.httpClient.Do`.

checks: Package compiles without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
No linter issues: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`
ExchangeCode tests pass: `go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -run 'TestExchangeCode'`
Requirements 07-REQ-3.1 through 07-REQ-3.E2 acceptance criteria met
Property test TS-07-P3 passes confirming grant_type always present

task_groups.title: Implement username derivation helper and GetUserInfo method

subtasks.title: Implement deriveUsername helper function
details: Implement `func deriveUsername(email string) (string, error)` (unexported helper).
Split on '@'; if no '@' or empty local part before '@', return error.
Remove all characters not matching `[0-9A-Za-z-]` using `regexp.MustCompile` or `strings.Map`.
If result is empty after sanitization, return descriptive error.
Truncate to 39 characters if longer.
Return sanitized string and nil error on success.

subtasks.title: Implement GetUserInfo — HTTP request, response decoding, and field mapping
details: Implement `func (p *GoogleProvider) GetUserInfo(accessToken string) (*UserInfo, error)`.
Build GET request to `p.userInfoURL` with `Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>` header.
Use `p.httpClient.Do(req)`; defer `resp.Body.Close()`.
On non-2xx status, return `(nil, fmt.Errorf("google userinfo endpoint returned status %d", resp.StatusCode))`.
Decode JSON into a struct with fields: ID (`id`), Email (`email`), Name (`name`).
Call `deriveUsername(googleResp.Email)` to populate Login; propagate any error as `(nil, error)`.
Return `&UserInfo{ID: googleResp.ID, Login: login, Email: googleResp.Email, Name: googleResp.Name}, nil`.

checks: Package compiles without errors: `go build ./internal/auth/...`
No linter issues: `go vet ./internal/auth/...`
All GetUserInfo and username derivation tests pass: `go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -run 'TestGetUserInfo|TestUsername'`
Property tests TS-07-P1 and TS-07-P4 pass confirming Login invariants
Requirements 07-REQ-4.1 through 07-REQ-5.E2 acceptance criteria met

task_groups.title: Update docs/configuration.md with Google OAuth setup instructions

subtasks.title: Add Google OAuth section to docs/configuration.md
details: Open `docs/configuration.md` and add a new section titled "Google OAuth".
Include: steps to create a Google OAuth 2.0 Client ID in Google Cloud Console.
Include: OAuth consent screen configuration (app name, scopes: openid, email, profile).
Include: Authorized redirect URIs setup (e.g. `http://localhost:8080/auth/callback/google` for dev mode).
Include: config.toml example with `[[oauth.providers]]`, `name = "google"`, `client_id = "..."`, `client_secret = "..."`.
Include: note about optional URL override fields.

task_groups.title: End-to-end wiring verification and smoke tests

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths — verify 07-PATH-1 (successful login) call chain has no stubs
details: Verify that callback handler in `internal/auth/handlers.go` calls `ExchangeCode` and `GetUserInfo` on the registered provider (existing, provider-agnostic handler — no changes needed).
Confirm `GoogleProvider.ExchangeCode` calls `p.httpClient.Do` (not a stub `panic("not implemented")`).
Confirm `GoogleProvider.GetUserInfo` calls `deriveUsername` and returns a fully-populated `*UserInfo`.
Grep for `panic("not implemented")`, `// TODO`, and bare `return nil` in non-trivial paths within `google_provider.go` — confirm none remain.

subtasks.title: Trace execution path 07-PATH-2 — empty username error propagation to HTTP 502
details: Confirm that when `deriveUsername` returns an error, `GetUserInfo` returns `(nil, error)` (not a zero-value `UserInfo`).
Confirm that the callback handler maps a non-nil `GetUserInfo` error to HTTP 502 (existing handler behavior, no new code needed — verify by code inspection).
Run TS-07-SMOKE-2: provide mock servers for token (success) and userinfo (email=="...@gmail.com"); assert callback responds 502.

subtasks.title: Trace execution path 07-PATH-3 — server startup wiring: config → switch → Registry
details: Confirm `cmd/af-hub/main.go` iterates over `cfg.OAuth.Providers`, matches `name=="google"` in the switch, and calls `auth.NewGoogleProvider(cfg)` (not a stub).
Confirm the returned `*GoogleProvider` is registered in the `Registry` under key `"google"`.
Run TS-07-SMOKE-3: start server with google provider config; assert providers API response contains name=="google"; assert no connection to actual Google servers.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation — access token and UserInfo flow through handler
details: Confirm the access token string returned by `ExchangeCode` is passed as argument to `GetUserInfo` in the callback handler.
Confirm `UserInfo.Login`, `UserInfo.ID`, `UserInfo.Email`, `UserInfo.Name` returned by `GetUserInfo` are all used by the handler (user record creation/update).
Inspect handler code in `handlers.go` to verify no field is silently discarded.

subtasks.title: Run all spec smoke tests and full test suite; confirm zero failures
details: Run `go test ./... -count=1` — zero failures.
Run `go test ./internal/auth/... -count=1 -v` — all GoogleProvider tests pass.
Run `go build ./cmd/af-hub/...` — binary builds successfully.
Run `go vet ./...` — zero warnings.
Confirm no `panic("not implemented")` or `// TODO` markers remain in `internal/auth/google_provider.go` or `cmd/af-hub/main.go`.
